title: The Cut Finger
content:
آ آ آ آ The Gossoon (Weeping)

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s bleedinï؟½! Itï؟½s bleedinï؟½!


آ آ آ آ The Ould Woman (Soothingly)

آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ shure, me lad, ï؟½t is bleedinï؟½;
آ آ آ آ But come, me hearty laddy buck, be brave anï؟½ do not cry;
آ آ آ آ A lad thatï؟½s learninï؟½ readinï؟½ shï؟½u'd be far beyant the heedinï؟½
آ آ آ آ Av a tiny bit oï؟½ finger cut that hurrts a bit foreby.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Ere ye come till wan anï؟½ twinty
آ آ آ آ Yeï؟½ll be havinï؟½ hurrts in plinty
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ yeï؟½ll learn a bit oï؟½ bleedinï؟½ doesnï؟½t mean yeï؟½re goinï؟½ tï؟½ die.


آ آ آ آ The Gossoon (Crying)

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s bleedinï؟½! Itï؟½s bleedinï؟½!


آ آ آ آ The Ould Woman (Comfortingly)

آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ shure, me lad, ï؟½t is bleedinï؟½;
آ آ آ آ But heï؟½s me slashinï؟½ buckeen, anï؟½ he will not weep at all;
آ آ آ آ A rag is all ï؟½t is needinï؟½ fer tï؟½ sthop the whole proceedinï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½, shure, a bit oï؟½ rosy blood wonï؟½t make me gossoon bawl;
آ آ آ آ Fer ï؟½t is but wan way av knowinï؟½
آ آ آ آ Ye have good red blood a-flowinï؟½
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ a-workinï؟½ all inside av ye tï؟½ make ye strong anï؟½ tall.


آ آ آ آ The Gossoon (Sobbing)

آ آ آ آ Itï؟½s bleedinï؟½! Itï؟½s bleedinï؟½!


آ آ آ آ The Ould Woman (Lovingly)

آ آ آ آ Aye, aye, me lad, ï؟½t is bleedinï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ some foine day yer hearrt will bleed as bleeds the hearrt av me.
آ آ آ آ The saints ye will be pleadinï؟½, but ï؟½t is little theyï؟½ll be heedinï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Fer the worrld is full av bleedinï؟½ hearrts on either side the sea.
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ Iï؟½d die tï؟½ aise the achinï؟½
آ آ آ آ Whin ye feel yer hearrt a-brealdnï؟½,
آ آ آ آ But, ah! the poor ould woman wonï؟½t be there tï؟½ comfort ye.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Ellis Parker Butler delivers a powerful performance in "The Cut Finger"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
